name: Austria

authority.name: Rundfunk und Telekom Regulierungs-GmbH (RTR) — supervisory authority for the Telekom-Control-Kommission (TKK), Austrian eIDAS supervisory body and national Trusted List operator
authority.operator: Accredited QTSPs under eIDAS (A-Trust GmbH, A-Trust Ges. f. Sicherheitssysteme im elektr. Datenverkehr GmbH, e-commerce monitoring GmbH / GlobalTrust, PrimeSign GmbH, Swisscom IT Services Finance S.E., SwissSign GmbH, and others)

governingLaw: Reglamento eIDAS (UE) 910/2014 · Signatur- und Vertrauensdienstegesetz (SVG) · Telekommunikationsgesetz 2021 (TKG 2021)

technology.trustList: ETSI TS 119 612 — the Austrian Trusted List (vertrauensliste.xml, RTR / TKK)

relatedLinks.label: EU LOTL — List of the Lists (European Commission)

notes: Austria's qualified-signature trust is a federation of accredited QTSPs (A-Trust GmbH and A-Trust Ges. f. Sicherheitssysteme im elektr. Datenverkehr GmbH, e-commerce monitoring GmbH / GlobalTrust, PrimeSign GmbH, Swisscom IT Services Finance S.E., and SwissSign GmbH, among others) supervised by Rundfunk und Telekom Regulierungs-GmbH (RTR), the supervisory authority acting for the Telekom-Control-Kommission (TKK), and published in the Austrian national eIDAS Trusted List (vertrauensliste.xml). The 39 currently-granted CA certificates were promoted wholesale after verifying the Trusted List's XAdES signature through the EU LOTL chain of trust: the LOTL signature was checked against the pinned European Commission signing anchor, and the Austrian TSL's signature (signer: C=AT, O=Rundfunk und Telekom Regulierungs-GmbH, OU=Fachbereich Telekommunikation und Post, CN=Trusted List 9; cert SHA-256 5a05d58ab2bcede1affb73024de8796c8c299fb37f2e79036c7e09f45712abe6) against the identity the LOTL declares for Austria, so the list's single seal vouches for every certificate it contains (eIDAS Art. 22). Only services with a currently-granted status and a CA / qualified-certificate service type were selected; withdrawn, deprecated, and non-CA services were dropped. Verification evidence is in VERIFICATION.md. Key algorithms across the set are mixed: 38 CAs use RSA-4096 and 1 uses RSA-2048. Three certificates in the set (a-sign-premium-mobile-05, a-sign-Premium-Sig-05, and EU-Identity-mobile-05) have passed their notAfter and are retained as archived-but-granted entries; they surface under the Expired status filter.
